auto_posts.title: Azure Local Update — February 5, 2026
auto_posts.subtitle: Documentation changes
auto_posts.content:
**Azure Local Documentation Updates: Key Changes for Operations Teams**

Microsoft has updated Azure Local deployment documentation with critical terminology changes. The SQL Server deployment guide now explicitly lists supported workload types including "Online transaction processing (OLTP) workloads" rather than the previous generic description. VM prerequisites now require "Azure role-based access control (RBAC)" instead of just "RBAC role," potentially affecting permission assignments.

Most importantly, references have changed from "Azure Local" to "Azure Local instance" throughout VM management sections. Teams must verify their automation scripts and runbooks align with this terminology, particularly when referencing custom locations that now "shows up on the **Overview** page for your Azure Local instance" rather than generic Azure Local references.

Update your deployment documentation and ensure team members use the precise RBAC terminology when configuring permissions.

Updated documentation: aks-create-clusters-portal • sql-server-23h2 • hci-vm-prerequisites • hci-vm-sign-in-set-subscription • create-arc-virtual-machines • whats-new
